steps.text: Estimate parameters of the calibration model from the responses of known, pure analytes.
Analyze multiple levels of calibration standards over a selected working range.
Replicate measurements at each calibrant level to evaluate random error.
Evaluate goodness-of-fit for various linear, curvilinear, or non-linear models.
Demonstrate that the instrument response is a first-order linear equation during intralaboratory method validation.
Include calibration study results in a method validation report.
Test the calibration approach during intralaboratory validation study.
Test according to the required/recommended calibration procedure during interlaboratory validation study.
Document the instrument calibration procedure used in the final EPA method in a method validation report.
Estimate method bias by measuring materials whose composition is reasonably well known.

preconditions.text: Before validating an analytical method, ensure that proper sample preservation and storage stability studies were performed during method development.
